rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Pod kv-store-2 is stuck Pending because its PVC data-kv-store-2 was manually recreated with storageClassName: broken-storage-class — a StorageClass that does not exist in the cluster. The only available StorageClass is lvms-vg1. Volume provisioning fails repeatedly (ProvisioningFailed, 18 events), leaving the PVC unbound and the pod unschedulable. The StatefulSet's OrderedReady policy blocks any further scaling until kv-store-2 recovers.
causalChain: Signal: KubeStatefulSetReplicasMismatch — StatefulSet kv-store has 3 desired replicas but only 2 ready
Why? Pod kv-store-2 is stuck in Pending state and never becomes Ready
Why? The scheduler cannot place kv-store-2 because its PVC data-kv-store-2 is unbound (FailedScheduling: pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims)

Why? PVC data-kv-store-2 references storageClassName: broken-storage-class which does not exist in the cluster (ProvisioningFailed: storageclass.storage.k8s.io 'broken-storage-class' not found, 18 occurrences)

Root cause: PVC data-kv-store-2 was manually re-created (manager: kubectl-create) with an incorrect/non-existent StorageClass 'broken-storage-class' instead of the cluster's only available StorageClass 'lvms-vg1'

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: The remediation target is PVC data-kv-store-2 because it is the specific resource with the misconfigured storageClassName. Deleting and recreating it with storageClassName: lvms-vg1 (or letting the StatefulSet controller recreate it) will unblock provisioning and allow kv-store-2 to schedule and become Ready. The StatefulSet itself does not need modification — its volumeClaimTemplate does not specify a storageClassName, so the controller would use the default (lvms-vg1).
dueDiligence.proportionality: The fix is surgical: delete and recreate PVC data-kv-store-2 with the correct StorageClass. This affects only the one broken PVC and does not require any changes to the StatefulSet, other PVCs, or cluster-level resources.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: StatefulSet spec and status, all 3 pods, all 3 PVCs, all StorageClasses in cluster, StatefulSet events, pod events for kv-store-2, PVC events for data-kv-store-2. Did not check: node conditions (not relevant since scheduling failure is clearly PVC-related), ConfigMap kv-store-config (not relevant to scheduling), network policies (not relevant).
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: replica mismatch → pod pending → PVC unbound → StorageClass not found → manual PVC recreation with wrong StorageClass. Cannot go deeper without knowing who/what issued the kubectl-create command.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: PVC spec confirmed storageClassName: broken-storage-class via kubectl_describe; StorageClass list confirms only lvms-vg1 exists; PVC events show ProvisioningFailed 18 times with exact error message; pod events confirm FailedScheduling due to unbound PVC; manager field in PVC managedFields confirms kubectl-create as creator (not StatefulSet controller).
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for this StatefulSet per get_namespaced_resource_context (tier1: [], tier2: []). N/A — no regression risk from prior remediations.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: 1) Node pressure/resource exhaustion: ruled out — kv-store-0 and kv-store-1 scheduled and running normally on cluster nodes; 2) Image pull failure: ruled out — image quay.io/kubernaut-cicd/demo-http-server:1.0.0 is present on nodes per events; 3) StorageClass provisioner failure (topolvm.io): ruled out — the provisioner successfully provisioned volumes for data-kv-store-0 and data-kv-store-1, and the issue is specifically the non-existent StorageClass name; 4) Quota/capacity limits: ruled out — other PVCs bound successfully at 300Mi each.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.97: Starting at 1.0. -0.02 for not knowing the exact operator/human who issued the kubectl-create command (audit log inaccessible). -0.01 for not verifying whether the StatefulSet's volumeClaimTemplate has an explicit storageClassName that might conflict with recreation.

contributingFactors: PVC data-kv-store-2 was manually recreated (kubectl-create) with storageClassName: broken-storage-class, overriding the correct lvms-vg1 StorageClass used by kv-store-0 and kv-store-1
broken-storage-class does not exist and has never existed in the cluster; only lvms-vg1 is available
The StatefulSet controller deleted the original kv-store-2 pod at 12:43:04Z; the replacement pod (recreated at 12:43:08Z) cannot schedule because the new PVC is unbound
ProvisioningFailed events repeated 18 times (12:43:07Z–12:47:10Z): storageclass.storage.k8s.io 'broken-storage-class' not found
StatefulSet podManagementPolicy is OrderedReady, so kv-store-2 must come up before any further scaling can proceed

kaResponse.analysis: Pod kv-store-2 is stuck Pending because its PVC data-kv-store-2 was manually recreated with storageClassName: broken-storage-class — a StorageClass that does not exist in the cluster. The only available StorageClass is lvms-vg1. Volume provisioning fails repeatedly (ProvisioningFailed, 18 events), leaving the PVC unbound and the pod unschedulable. The StatefulSet's OrderedReady policy blocks any further scaling until kv-store-2 recovers.
